title: Doctors Get Their Day!
story: Every March 30, people across the country celebrate National Doctors Day to thank the medical heroes who keep us healthy! Hospitals hang special banners, patients send thank-you cards, and families bring flowers or treats to their favorite doctors. Some medical offices get flooded with homemade cookies and grateful notes from kids who want to show appreciation for their pediatricians.
activity_question: How do people commonly show appreciation to doctors on National Doctors Day?
activity_choices: Send thank-you cards and bring treats
activity_answer: Send thank-you cards and bring treats
follow_up_question: What would you write in a thank-you note to your doctor?

title: Slurp Up Something Strange!
story: Turkey neck soup might sound weird, but March 30 is the day to give it a try! This unusual holiday celebrates a soup made from turkey necks that creates a rich, flavorful broth. People who celebrate this quirky food day visit restaurants that serve the soup or make it at home using family recipes. The turkey necks are simmered for hours to make the soup extra tasty and nutritious.
activity_question: What makes turkey neck soup especially flavorful?
activity_choices: The necks are simmered for hours
activity_answer: The necks are simmered for hours
follow_up_question: What other unusual food holidays would you like to try celebrating?

title: Step Into Fun: Park Walking Day
story: Every March 30, people celebrate National Take a Walk in the Park Day by doing exactly what it sounds like! Families pack snacks and head to their favorite parks for peaceful strolls. Some people bring their dogs, others carry cameras to snap photos of spring flowers and wildlife. Parks fill with walkers of all ages enjoying fresh air and nature. It's the perfect excuse to put down phones, step outside, and explore the green spaces in your neighborhood.
activity_question: What do many people bring with them when celebrating this holiday?
follow_up_question: What's the most interesting thing you've ever discovered while walking in a park?

title: Sharp Celebration for National Pencil Day
story: Every March 30, people celebrate National Pencil Day by honoring the simple yellow writing tool that helps us create amazing things! Students bring their favorite pencils to school and teachers share fun facts about how pencils are made from wood and graphite. Artists post drawings made entirely with pencils on social media, showing off incredible sketches and detailed artwork. Some people even collect vintage pencils or visit pencil factories to see how these everyday heroes are crafted.
activity_question: What do artists often post on social media during National Pencil Day?
follow_up_question: What's the most creative thing you've ever drawn or written with a pencil?
